intro_en: Tools form the interface between the model's probabilistic decisions and the external world, so their design, permissions, and feedback set the ceiling for an agentic system. Code expands this interface into a meta-capability: an agent can formalize reasoning, create adapters and interfaces, and accumulate verifiable procedures.

concepts.explanation_en: An ACI translates an LLM's intent into action through an interface designed around the model's own limitations. Its quality depends on granularity, degree of generality, guidance about when to use it, fidelity in passing parameters, and observable feedback. A general-purpose tool reduces the choice space and covers unforeseen cases, but dangerous actions require narrow permissions and auditing. The author proposes choosing between a dedicated tool and a Skill plus a general executor along three dimensions: parameter complexity, frequency of change, and model capability. Document formats with similar interfaces are combined under `read_document`; OCR and video analysis remain separate. A `code_interpreter` is preferable to a collection of calculators, while writing to a production database is a separate tool. A description must state boundaries and negative examples; the framework must not transform arguments invisibly. Analytical extrapolation: version each tool's JSON Schema and run contract tests against representative trajectories before release.
concepts.analogy_en: An ACI is like an aircraft instrument panel: what matters is not having the largest possible number of buttons, but an unambiguous mapping between the pilot's intent, the control, and the observable outcome.

concepts.explanation_en: MCP standardizes how clients interact with servers that provide tools, resources, and prompts, but it does not solve choice overload. Skills turn hundreds of narrow capabilities into a small set of general executors with instructions loaded on demand, while dynamic discovery adds only relevant definitions to the context. The book describes local stdio and remote Streamable HTTP, emphasizing that MCP specifies the protocol for an individual interaction rather than a complete scheduler for cross-session tasks. Five servers can consume roughly 55,000 tokens; in the cited Anthropic result, retrieving definitions on demand raised Opus 4 tool-use accuracy from 49% to 74%. At the same time, a third-party server introduces risks such as description poisoning, tool shadowing, and misuse of OAuth tokens and API keys, so review, version pinning, least privilege, and a runtime gate are required. Analytical extrapolation: maintain a provenance registry and prevent an untrusted server from overriding the name of an already registered tool.
concepts.analogy_en: MCP is a standard electrical outlet, Skills are a catalog of appliance manuals, and dynamic discovery is a librarian who brings only the manual you need.
concepts.visual_title_en: Visual model: MCP, Skills, and Dynamic Tool Discovery

concepts.explanation_en: A production agent must accept external events while it is working, change priorities, cancel or queue tasks, and resume after an interruption. This conflicts with the synchronous format used to train LLMs, in which a tool call must be followed immediately by its result, so the framework must hide asynchrony behind a robust event protocol. PineClaw illustrates the limits of Heartbeat: in the author's illustrative scenario, polling at an interval of, for example, five minutes detects a user's request during a call too slowly. A Channel turns callbacks into push events and reduces latency from minutes to seconds. User messages, tool returns, webhooks, and timers are consolidated into a single structured stream. Analytical extrapolation: maintain a durable event log with correlation IDs and idempotent replay so the causal chain can be reconstructed after a failure.
concepts.analogy_en: It is a dispatch center receiving calls, sensor alerts, and schedules at the same time: the dispatcher does not wait for one conversation to end before noticing a fire alarm.

concepts.explanation_en: A Coding Agent plus a filesystem forms a general-purpose core for open-ended tasks: the model explores the environment, modifies artifacts, and leaves behind reproducible state. The harness turns this freedom into a controlled loop by defining an acceptance baseline, execution boundaries, fast feedback signals, rollback, and finite recovery conditions. The minimal toolset includes a code interpreter, Bash, read, write, edit, glob, and grep. Files become working memory and an audit trail, while process state can be recovered from the repository. A typical loop reads AGENTS.md-like instructions, clarifies requirements, aligns on the design of a complex task, implements it, tests, repairs, performs a self-review, and updates documentation. Reliability is provided by tests, linters, types, Git, structured tool errors, retry classification, repeated-call fingerprints, an idle watchdog, a recovery ceiling, and a circuit breaker. Analytical extrapolation: track recovery rate, repeated-call rate, and forced termination as separate agent SLOs.
concepts.analogy_en: The model is a test pilot, while the harness is a proving ground with barriers, instruments, an emergency brake, and a log of every maneuver.
concepts.visual_title_en: Visual model: Coding Agent, Filesystem, and Harness Engineering

concepts.explanation_en: Code separates natural-language understanding from exact computation and turns ambiguous rules into deterministic constraints. The LLM formalizes the task, while an interpreter, symbolic engine, or server-side validator computes the result from data the model cannot falsify. In the problem involving 40 students, textual reasoning confuses the set calculation and produces 14 instead of 8, whereas Python makes the arithmetic verifiable. For mathematics, the book recommends SymPy, NumPy, and SciPy; for logic problems, a constraint solver. The airline ticket cancellation policy has three layers: natural-language rules for explanation, `expected_*` parameters as a reasoning checklist, and a server-side gatekeeper based on database truth and the server clock. Analytical extrapolation: test business rules against a historical corpus and monitor branch coverage before changing the policy.
concepts.analogy_en: Natural language is an explanation of traffic rules, while code is the barrier gate: the former aids understanding, while the latter physically prevents a violation from passing through.
concepts.visual_title_en: Visual model: Code as Formal Reasoning and Executable Policy

concepts.explanation_en: Code is a meta-capability because it creates new capabilities, constraints, and forms of expression on demand. It connects incompatible systems, produces multimedia artifacts and interfaces, and, at the limit, allows an agent to repair and create other agents from verified scaffolds. For presentations, video, and visualizations, a Proposer writes Slidev, Blender, or FFmpeg code; a Reviewer examines the actual render and returns structured feedback. A system adapter generates an HTTP client and transformations for unstable formats; an adaptive log parser repairs the frontend after seeing a new sample. The Artifact pattern sends SQL to a system executor and moves data from the database to the UI without forcing the LLM to recount thousands of rows; generated SQL requires a separate security layer with read-only permissions, validation, and isolation. Analytical extrapolation: sign the source scaffold, build the descendant in isolation, and withhold real credentials until it passes a mandatory evaluation gate.
concepts.analogy_en: This is not a single ready-made key, but a machine that cuts a new key from a blueprint, tests it on a bench, and, when needed, builds the next machine from a reference design.
concepts.visual_title_en: Visual model: Code as a Generative Adapter and Bootstrapping Mechanism
